observations.topic: Good advice can still lose on price
observations.what_customers_say: Some customers praise consultation quality but still select another provider because of price or offer terms.
observations.decision_relevance: Positive sentiment after a call does not mean the customer is ready to sign if a competing offer exists.

observations.what_customers_say: Known referrals can outweigh provider claims or otherwise good consultation.
observations.decision_relevance: A customer comparing against a referred installer may need credible local proof, not generic persuasion.

observations.what_customers_say: Customers value quick and knowledgeable answers while evaluating an offer.
observations.decision_relevance: Unanswered questions can turn a warm lead into a stalled lead.

observations.what_customers_say: Marketplace buyers often think in terms of alternatives, comparisons, and provider fit.
observations.decision_relevance: The quote follow-up should help the customer compare apples to apples.

observations.what_customers_say: Reviewers may separate liking the advice from the final buying decision.
observations.decision_relevance: A strategy based only on satisfaction signals can miss the real blocker.

cross_source_notes: Price and referral competition appears across multiple public German solar sources.
Fast answer language aligns with other positive review surfaces.
caveats: Review text does not reveal full quote scope or competitor terms.
Use for qualitative buyer language, not price benchmarks.
